data.narrative:
Black Forest Labs is a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Robin Rombach, Andreas Blattmann, and Patrick Esser, currently valued above $1B with $300M in total capital raised. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom) plus 1 public comparable, spanning a wide range from $500M to $32.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($300M) as a proxy baseline with a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies a valuation of $570M. Peer-median analysis across the five comparables yields a median valuation of $5.0B, with a range from $500M (Photoroom) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence). The wide dispersion reflects differentiated risk and scale assumptions within the frontier AI cohort.

These methods triangulate to a range of $484M to $5.8B, with a median of $2.8B. The current valuation claim of >$1B sits within this range but toward the lower bound. Key uncertainties include the appropriate weighting of outlier comparables—particularly Safe Superintelligence's $32B valuation—and whether Black Forest Labs' generative modeling capabilities command the premium implied by the peer median relative to stage-progression mechanics.
